title: Compare Physical Tote, Robot, And Rack Labels To RMS Map Identifiers
summary: Use the RMS map and physical label visibility or reported physical observations to verify that tote, robot, and rack position labels match the identifiers shown in the system map.
when_to_use: Use when verifying whether a physical tote, robot, or rack position matches the identifier shown in the RMS map, including investigation of possible tote identity, robot identity, rack identity, or physical-to-system mismatch conditions.
not_for: Correcting mismatches when the physical label does not match the RMS map identifier
Executing robot removal, robot add, tote swap, tote remove, or tote re-add actions
Any complete recovery workflow for mismatch cases not provided in this source

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Check physical labels or use reported physical observations and compare them to identifiers shown in the RMS map.

safety_notes: This source supports comparison and verification activity only; it does not provide a complete corrective procedure for mismatch cases.
Do not use this runbook as authority to perform recovery actions not explicitly supported by this source.
access_or_tools_needed: Visibility of physical rack position labels

access_required: Access to the RMS web application / RMS map
system_preconditions: The tote, robot, or rack position to be checked is known or can be located in the physical area or by reported physical observation.

visual_references.description: Training frame showing rack location and container lookup concepts, including that clicking a dark blue rack shows which tote is on that rack and supports comparison of physical labels to map state.

steps.title: Go to or obtain the physical observation
steps.purpose: Establish the physical item or location being verified before comparing it to the RMS map.
steps.instruction: Go to the physical area for the tote, robot, or rack position being checked, or use reported physical observations for that item or location.
steps.expected_result: The operator has a specific tote, robot, or rack position selected for comparison.
steps.healthy_condition: The physical item or location to be checked is clearly identified.
steps.failure_condition: The operator cannot determine which physical tote, robot, or rack position is being checked.

actions.label: Identify physical item or location
actions.exact_action: Go to the physical area or use reported physical observations for the tote, robot, or rack position being checked.

stop_or_escalate_if: The physical item or location cannot be identified well enough to compare against the RMS map.

source_evidence.text: Go to the physical area or use reported physical observations for the tote, robot, or rack position being checked.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Every tote has a label, the robot has a label number, and every rack position has a label that should match what's in the map.

steps.title: Read the physical label
steps.purpose: Capture the physical identifier that will be compared to the RMS map.
steps.instruction: Read the visible label on the tote, robot, or rack position.
steps.expected_result: A physical label value is available for comparison.
steps.healthy_condition: The label is visible and readable on the tote, robot, or rack position.
steps.failure_condition: The label is missing, obstructed, unreadable, or cannot be confirmed from the available observation.

actions.label: Read physical label
actions.exact_action: Read the visible label on the tote, robot, or rack position.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use as an example reference for rack and tote lookup context while identifying the physical item to compare with the map.

stop_or_escalate_if: The physical label cannot be read or confirmed.

source_evidence.text: Read the visible label on the tote, robot, or rack position.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Every tote has a label, the robot has a label number, and every rack position has a label that should match what's in the map.
The transcript explains rack locations in the map/RMS view... and mismatches between physical labels and system state.

steps.title: Open the RMS map and navigate to the item
steps.purpose: Bring up the system view that shows the identifier for the tote, robot, or rack position being checked.
steps.instruction: Open the RMS map and navigate to the corresponding location or selected object.
steps.expected_result: The relevant tote, robot, or rack position is visible in the RMS map.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator can access the RMS map and locate the corresponding object or location.
steps.failure_condition: The RMS map cannot be accessed or the corresponding object or location cannot be located.

actions.label: Open RMS map
actions.exact_action: Open the RMS map and navigate to the corresponding location or selected object.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Reference that RMS is the robot management system web application used to investigate system state.
Reference rack selection and map lookup behavior for locating the relevant rack or tote in the map.

stop_or_escalate_if: The corresponding location or object cannot be found in the map.

source_evidence.text: Open the RMS map and navigate to the corresponding location or selected object.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Users access the RMS web application through a unique IP address with username and password... and can use it to investigate problems.
Selecting a dark blue rack in the map shows the tote associated with that rack.

steps.title: Read the identifier shown in the map
steps.purpose: Capture the system-reported identifier for comparison against the physical label.
steps.instruction: Read the identifier shown in the map for the tote, robot, or rack position.
steps.expected_result: A system-reported identifier is available from the RMS map.
steps.healthy_condition: The map clearly shows the identifier for the selected tote, robot, or rack position.
steps.failure_condition: The map does not show a usable identifier for the selected item or location.

actions.label: Read map identifier
actions.exact_action: Read the identifier shown in the map for the tote, robot, or rack position.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: If checking a rack, use the map behavior where clicking a dark blue rack shows which tote is on that rack.
Container Selection screen example for locating a tote, rack, or AGV and reading the selected item details.

stop_or_escalate_if: The RMS map does not display the identifier needed for comparison.

source_evidence.text: Read the identifier shown in the map for the tote, robot, or rack position.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Every tote has a label, the robot has a label number, and every rack position has a label that should match what's in the map.
When a rack is dark blue, if you click on it, it will tell you what tote you have on the rack itself.

steps.title: Compare the physical label to the map identifier
steps.purpose: Determine whether the physical item identity matches the system-reported identity.
steps.instruction: Compare the physical label to the identifier shown in the map.
steps.expected_result: The operator determines whether the physical and system identifiers match.
steps.healthy_condition: The physical label matches the identifier shown in the RMS map.
steps.failure_condition: The physical label does not match the identifier shown in the RMS map.

actions.label: Compare identifiers
actions.exact_action: Compare the physical label to the identifier shown in the map.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Use the rack/tote lookup context to compare what is physically present with what the map reports.

stop_or_escalate_if: The physical label does not match the map identifier.

source_evidence.text: Compare the physical label to the identifier shown in the map.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Every tote has a label, the robot has a label number, and every rack position has a label that should match what's in the map.

steps.title: Record any mismatch
steps.purpose: Document the result of the comparison so the mismatch can be escalated or handled by a separate source-backed procedure.
steps.instruction: Record any mismatch between the physical label and the system-reported identifier.
steps.expected_result: Any mismatch is documented for follow-up.
steps.healthy_condition: No mismatch is found, or any mismatch found is clearly recorded.
steps.failure_condition: A mismatch exists but is not documented, or the operator attempts unsupported corrective action from this source alone.

actions.label: Document mismatch
actions.exact_action: Record any mismatch between the physical label and the system-reported identifier.

stop_or_escalate_if: A mismatch is found between the physical label and the map identifier.
A corrective action is needed that is not provided in this source.

source_evidence.text: Record any mismatch between the physical label and the system-reported identifier.
Escalate or continue with a separate source-backed recovery procedure if the physical label does not match the map identifier.
This segment does not provide a complete corrective procedure for all mismatch cases.

success_criteria: The physical tote, robot, or rack position label is compared against the identifier shown in the RMS map.
The operator can confirm whether the physical and system identifiers match.
Any mismatch is recorded for follow-up.
healthy_conditions: The physical label is visible and readable.
The RMS map can be accessed and the corresponding item or location can be found.
The identifier shown in the RMS map matches the physical label.
failure_conditions: The physical label cannot be read or confirmed.
The RMS map cannot be accessed or does not show the needed identifier.
The physical label does not match the identifier shown in the RMS map.
A corrective action is required but is not provided by this source.
escalation_guidance: Escalate or continue with a separate source-backed recovery procedure if the physical label does not match the map identifier.
Do not infer or perform unsupported corrective actions from this training segment alone because it does not provide a complete corrective procedure for all mismatch cases.
